source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Other Lifting

event.top_event: Hand caught between crane cable and sheave resulting in finger lacerations and partial amputation
actions_taken: Injured person evacuated for medical treatment
All operations shut down for investigation
Stand-down held focusing on line of fire and hand placement during crane operations
Additional wire rope slings added to rigging to prevent crane block from being lowered through deck opening
Personnel reminded to keep radio communications clear and use buddy system
BSEE Houma District notified orally and written report submitted within 15 days
event.summary: On July 20, 2024, at 2200 hours, a rigger working on abandonment operations at Ship Shoal 224 D platform suffered lacerations to his ring finger and partial amputation of his pinky finger when his right hand was caught between the crane cable and crane block sheave while guiding a casing cutter tool through the deck. The incident occurred during a blind lift operation where the injured person used his hands instead of push poles to guide the suspended load, contrary to the operator's Material Handling policy. The JSAs performed that night did not identify the hazard of being caught between the crane cable and sheave, and Stop Work Authority was not invoked when the unsafe hand placement began.
key_phrases: hand caught between crane cable and sheave
Stop Work Authority not used initially

hazards.name: Crane cable and sheave pinch point

threats.name: Hand placed in line of fire between crane cable and sheave
Lack of supervision enforcement of safe work practices

consequences.name: Hand laceration and partial finger amputation

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.barrier_role: Identify hazards and safe work procedures

supporting_text: JSA's were completed for Abrasive Cutting Operations, Crane Ops for night shift 1800-0600, General Work on Deck, and Removing Conductors. Although blind lifts and hand placement was discussed in the pre-tour meeting, these hazards were not identified in any JSA used that night.
The JSA's did not identify the hazard of being caught between the crane's cable and sheave.

controls.name: Safe Operating Practices (TSOP-PCD-50) Material Handling policy - use of push poles
controls.barrier_role: Prevent hand placement in line of fire during load guidance

supporting_text: The operator's Material Handling policy specifies using push poles to guide suspended loads.
The operator did not enforce its Safe Operating Practices (TSOP-PCD-50) Material Handling and the IP did not follow this policy.
The IP guiding the load with his hands contributed significantly to this incident.

Stop Work Authority (SWA) was not used when the IP started guiding the load with his hands instead of a push pole. SWA was used, but only after the IP's hand was caught between the crane's cable and sheave.

Another member of the casing cutting team heard the IP yell out in pain. When he noticed the IP's hand caught in the sheave, he ran over to another team member with a radio and called for an all stop.

controls.name: Supervision and enforcement of safe work practices
controls.barrier_role: Ensure compliance with Material Handling policy and safe work procedures

supporting_text: Supervision – The operator did not enforce the use of push poles to guide suspended loads as per their policy - Safe Operating Practices (TSOP-PCD-50) Material Handling.

controls.name: Radio communication for blind lifts
controls.barrier_role: Enable coordination and awareness during blind lift operations

supporting_text: All lifts were blind lifts due to the lift boat being jacked-up to the top of its legs. The top deck of the platform was higher than the crane cab on the lift boat's crane, therefore all lifts required the use of radios.

controls.name: Positioning of riggers on different decks
controls.barrier_role: Monitor crane cable, block, slings and umbilical to prevent rubbing on grating and I-beams

supporting_text: The IP and R1 were posted in these positions to keep the crane cable, crane block, wire rope slings and umbilical from rubbing on the grating and I-beams on each deck.
The IP was using his hands to guide the crane block through the deck opening when his ring finger and pinky finger on his right hand were caught between the crane cable and the crane block sheave.

work.procedures_value: inadequate JSA and non-enforcement of Material Handling policy
work.tools_equipment_value: push poles available but not used

organisation.supervision_value: inadequate enforcement of safe work practices
organisation.training_value: JSA training inadequate regarding blind lifts and pinch points

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
